title: Iodes seguinii
uses: Iodes seguinii is a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, although specific uses and effects are not well-documented. However, more research is required to validate the plant's efficacy and potential health benefits. Further studies are needed to fully explore the medicinal potential of Iodes seguinii.

intro: Iodes seguinii, also known as the French or Asian yew, is a species of flowering plant in the family Icacinaceae. The French yew has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Iodes seguinii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Iodes seguinii is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions to treat colds, fever, and respiratory infections.
